summary:
Bone thickness and inter-radicular spacing critically determine whether successful mini-screw insertion can occur in posterior mandibular areas due to limited available space; accurate assessment via imaging helps guide safe surgical decision-making by evaluating structural constraints before implantation. These factors must be considered during treatment planning because inadequate bone or proximity risks compromise stability and success rates.
